source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Umbilical Damage and Chemical Release

actions_taken: All Stop called to inspect winch
event.summary: On 21-Sept-2015 at approximately 1745 hours, a chemical release occurred at Anadarko Petroleum's East Breaks 643 Boomvang SPAR during umbilical lowering operations. The umbilical hung on the hard tank umbilical guide, resulting in a breach and release of approximately 41 barrels of methanol and ethylene glycol. Biological marine growth caused umbilical centralizers to become stuck, causing the pull head to lie over at an angle and impact the hard tank guide, cutting the umbilical and three inner tubes.
recommendations: No recommendations issued by Lake Jackson District
key_phrases: umbilical hung on hard tank guide
pull head lay over at angle

hazards.name: Pressurized umbilical containing methanol and ethylene glycol
Subsurface hard tank umbilical guide structure
Biological marine growth on subsurface equipment

threats.name: Umbilical centralizers stuck due to marine biological growth
Umbilical pull head misalignment and impact with hard tank guide
Inability to detect umbilical load hang-up during lowering

consequences.name: Umbilical breach and rupture of inner tubes
Release of 41 barrels of methanol and ethylene glycol to marine environment
Environmental pollution - water soluble chemical dispersal

controls.name: Umbilical centralizers
controls.barrier_role: Guide and protect umbilical during subsurface lowering
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical control to prevent umbilical contact with hard tank guide

supporting_text: The umbilical was equipped with centralizers to help guide it through the hard tank segmented tubes
Some of the centralizers are believed to have become stuck as a result of biological marine growth

controls.name: Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Visual detection of umbilical hang-up and damage during lowering
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to identify problems during operation

supporting_text: The lowering operation was being monitored by a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV), which was positioned at the bottom of the hard tank section of the SPAR at a depth of approximately 500'
During the lowering operation, the umbilical was observed via the ROV to quickly drop 10' to 15'
An 'All Stop' was called to inspect the winch for damage

controls.name: Versabuild VB50-50 winch load indicator or tension monitoring system
controls.barrier_role: Alert winch operator to abnormal load conditions indicating hang-up
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to identify load anomalies

supporting_text: The winch operator was unable to determine the umbilical (load) had become hung on the hard tank umbilical guides because the Versabuild VB50-50 winch was not equipped with a load indicator or tension monitoring system
The Versabuild VB50-50 winch was not equipped with a weight indicator or tension monitoring system to alert the winch operator the umbilical (load) was not descending

controls.name: Visual confirmation by winch operator or ROV of subsurface umbilical guides
controls.barrier_role: Direct observation of umbilical path and guide interaction
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control for subsurface operations

supporting_text: The umbilical guides are subsurface and located within the hard tank of the SPAR, therefore visual confirmation was not possible with the ROV or by the winch operator

controls.name: Maintenance and cleaning of subsurface umbilical guides to prevent biological growth
controls.barrier_role: Prevent biological marine growth from degrading centralizer function
controls.lod_basis: Primary preventive control to maintain equipment condition

supporting_text: Biological marine growth caused the umbilical centralizers to become stuck on the hard tank umbilical guide
Some of the centralizers are believed to have become stuck as a result of biological marine growth

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
